title: The Placebo Effect
puzzle: Why can someone feel better even when a treatment has no active drug in it?
insight: The placebo effect shows that expectation, context, and belief can influence symptoms and perception, even when the treatment itself is inert.
key_ideas: Placebos can affect pain and other subjective symptoms.
Expectation is part of the treatment experience.
Researchers use placebos to measure treatment effects more clearly.
mind_blower: Sometimes the body’s response to the idea of treatment becomes part of the treatment story.
next_curiosity: If expectation changes symptoms, what does that reveal about the mind-body connection?
narration_text: Why can someone feel better even when a treatment has no active drug in it? The placebo effect shows that expectation, context, and belief can influence symptoms and perception, even when the treatment itself is inert. Placebos can affect pain and other subjective symptoms. Expectation is part of the treatment experience. Researchers use placebos to measure treatment effects more clearly. Sometimes the body’s response to the idea of treatment becomes part of the treatment story. If expectation changes symptoms, what does that reveal about the mind-body connection?
